scenarios.opening_question: Tell me about yourself and your journey to internal medicine.
follow_up_pool: Why internal medicine specifically?
What has been your most challenging clinical experience?
Tell me about a time you handled a difficult patient interaction.
Describe a situation where you had to work as part of a team under pressure.
What do you consider your greatest professional strength?

scenarios.opening_question: Walk me through a time you made a clinical error and what you learned from it.
follow_up_pool: How did you disclose the error to the patient or family?
What systems-based changes did you advocate for after this incident?
How do you ensure quality and safety in your daily practice?
Tell me about a time you disagreed with a senior colleague.

scenarios.opening_question: Describe a patient who had a significant impact on you as a physician.
follow_up_pool: What did that experience teach you about the doctor-patient relationship?
How do you handle emotional situations in medicine — do you feel it affects your clinical judgment?
Tell me about a time you had to deliver difficult news to a patient.
What self-care strategies do you use to prevent burnout?

scenarios.opening_question: Why did you choose to apply to residency in the United States rather than staying in your home country?
follow_up_pool: What specific aspects of US medicine drew you here?
Do you plan to stay in the US long-term or return to your home country?
How will your international background benefit our program?
What challenges do you anticipate adapting to US clinical culture?

scenarios.opening_question: What are your career goals after residency — where do you see yourself in 10 years?
follow_up_pool: Are you considering a fellowship? Which subspecialty and why?
How does this program fit into your long-term career plan?
What research or academic interests do you have?
How would you contribute to our program beyond clinical duties?
